name: Tomatillo-Chipotle Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 canned chipotle chiles in adobo sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash tomatillos under hot water. Cook on a comal or black iron skillet (dry) for 20-25 minutes over medium-high heat until soft and blackened all over. Do not let them dry out; shake the pan every few minutes.
2. Roast garlic until it is soft but not burnt. Sauté onion in 1 tablespoon olive oil until soft and browned.
3. Add tomatillos, garlic, onion, remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil, chipotles, adobo sauce, cilantro, and salt to a blender or food processor. Blend until smooth with no lumps. If needed, add water for consistency.
4. Add lime juice and blend again. Adjust the amount of cilantro if desired. Serve warm, at room temperature, or chilled.
Yield: About 3 cups
Notes: This versatile sauce can be served hot or cold. It pairs well with roast pork sandwiches, grilled fish, pork, rabbit, lamb, or chicken, and is ideal for enchiladas. It has a milder flavor than regular tomato sauces. If served warm, do not let it sit too long as the cilantro may lose its color and become less flavorful.
